section_name: Regulation of Railroad by Type a General-law Municipality
chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Mun. Streets

subsections.text: The governing body of a Type A general-law municipality may:

direct and control the location and construction of railroad tracks, turnouts, and switches and prohibit the construction of those facilities in a street or alley, unless that action has been authorized by law;

require that railroad tracks, turnouts, and switches be constructed in a way that interferes as little as possible with the ordinary use of a street or alley and that leaves sufficient space on each side of the tracks for the safe and convenient passage of vehicles and people;
require a railroad company to keep in repair the street or alley on which their tracks are located;
order a railroad company to construct and keep in repair a crossing at the place where the company’s tracks intersect a street or alley;
require a railroad company to construct and keep in repair a ditch, sewer, or culvert;
direct or prohibit the use of or regulate the speed of a locomotive in the municipality; or
direct and control the location of railroad depots in the municipality.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
